messages.content:
Set into tv show The Arrow universe. 
Ta-er al-Sahfer is Sara Lance
Sarab is Maseo Yamashiko
Al Mansi is Nicolas
Ta-er al-Sahfer and Al Mansi were both rescued along the same time by Nysa Al ghul. Both women find themselves interested in him. He is a shy, young man. His dedication to training is second to none and he is making progress faster than anyone before him. He is also someone who should easily be forgotten. His unassuming nature make him someone nobody really would think as an assassin. Story start with Al Mansi in the training halls. He as progressed by leaps and bound and is on part with Sara in hand to hand combat with a style leaning more on hard style. He is now Working on his sword fighting, using a kris broadsword. Both Nyssa and Sara propose to help him. Nyssa proposes to start training in long range weapons like the bow and arrow. Training session. Written like fiction book.No declaration of romantic interest between Nyssa, Sara & Al Mansi.

Al Mansi stood in the training halls, his eyes focused on the task at hand. He held his kris broadsword tightly, his muscles bulging with the effort of his training. Sweat dripped down his forehead as he executed his moves with precision and grace.

It had been several months since he had been rescued by Nyssa Al Ghul and brought to the League of Assassins. Since then, he had dedicated himself to the art of fighting, determined to become the most skilled warrior he could be.

As he practiced, he noticed a presence in the room. Turning, he saw Nyssa and Sara, both standing there with their arms crossed.

"Good afternoon, Ta-er al-Sahfer," Nyssa said, a smile playing at the corners of her lips. "We've been watching your progress, and we're very impressed."

Al Mansi bowed respectfully. "Thank you, Lady Nyssa. I have been working hard to improve my skills."

Sara stepped forward. "We were actually talking, and we thought we could help you with your sword fighting. Nyssa could teach you some long-range weapons, like the bow and arrow."

Al Mansi's eyes widened in surprise. "That would be an honor, Lady Sara. I would be grateful for any help you could offer."

And so the training began. Nyssa took Al Mansi under her wing, teaching him the intricacies of the bow and arrow. Sara worked with him on sword fighting, showing him techniques that he had never even heard of before.

Days turned into weeks, and weeks turned into months. Al Mansi continued to improve, his dedication to training unmatched by anyone else in the League.

And as he trained, both Nyssa and Sara found themselves drawn to him, intrigued by his unassuming nature and his unwavering determination.

But for now, they simply focused on the task at hand, honing Al Mansi's skills and preparing him for whatever battles lay ahead.
Al Mansi continue to hone his skills, becoming on par with Al Owal in sword fighting.

With each passing day, Al Mansi grew more skilled in combat. He continued to train tirelessly, determined to become the best warrior he could possibly be.

Under Sara's tutelage, Al Mansi gained a mastery of the sword that quickly put him on par with Al Owal, one of the deadliest assassins in the League. Al Owal was known for his fierce fighting style and his skill with his scimitars, but Al Mansi's proficiency with the kris broadsword proved to be a match for him.

The two warriors often sparred together in the training halls, their swords clashing in a fierce dance that left those around them in awe. They pushed each other to their limits, testing their skills and honing their techniques.

Nyssa watched with pride as Al Mansi continued to improve. She had taken a special interest in him, recognizing his potential as a warrior and a member of the League. And as she watched him spar with Al Owal, she knew that he was destined for greatness.

But even as Al Mansi grew stronger, he remained humble and unassuming. He never let his newfound skill go to his head, always striving to improve and become even better.

And as he continued to train, he knew that he was one step closer to achieving his ultimate goal: to become the greatest warrior the League of Assassins had ever seen.
Al Mansi is now ready for his first mission as a member of the League of Assassins, he is to be part of a team with Nyssa, Sara and Sarab, a new member of the league.

Al Mansi was both nervous and excited as he prepared for his first mission as a member of the League of Assassins. He had been chosen to be part of a team with Nyssa, Sara, and Sarab, a new member of the League who had quickly proven himself to be a skilled warrior.

The mission was simple but crucial: they were to infiltrate a heavily guarded facility and retrieve a valuable artifact that had fallen into the wrong hands. The team had spent weeks planning their approach and preparing for the mission, and they were confident in their ability to succeed.

As they arrived at the facility under the cover of darkness, Al Mansi felt a surge of adrenaline course through his veins. He knew that this was what he had been training for, and he was ready to give it his all.

The team moved swiftly and silently, taking out guards and avoiding detection as they made their way deeper into the facility. Al Mansi was in the lead, his sword at the ready as he scanned the area for any signs of trouble.

They soon reached the room where the artifact was being held, and Al Mansi could feel his heart pounding in his chest. This was it. This was the moment he had been waiting for.

As they entered the room, they were met with unexpected resistance. A team of highly trained mercenaries stood between them and their objective, weapons at the ready.

Al Mansi and the rest of the team quickly sprang into action, engaging the mercenaries in a fierce battle of skill and strength. Al Mansi's sword flashed in the darkness, his moves fluid and precise as he struck down his opponents with ease.

In the end, the team emerged victorious, the artifact safely in their possession. Al Mansi could feel his heart swelling with pride as he looked around at his fellow warriors, realizing that he was now one of them – a member of the League of Assassins.

As they made their way back to their base, Al Mansi knew that this was only the beginning. There would be many more missions, many more battles to fight. But he was ready. He was a warrior, and he was part of a team that he knew he could always count on.
As they return to Nanda Parbat, Al Mansi seek Sarab so he can progressed in his concealment skills.

Upon their return to Nanda Parbat, Al Mansi sought out Sarab to help him improve his concealment skills. Sarab had already established himself as one of the most skilled members of the League in this area, and Al Mansi knew that he could learn a great deal from him.

Sarab welcomed Al Mansi with open arms, impressed by the progress he had already made in his combat skills. He knew that Al Mansi had the potential to become a truly great assassin, and he was eager to help him achieve his goals.

Together, they spent countless hours practicing the art of concealment. Sarab taught Al Mansi how to move silently, how to blend into the shadows, and how to use the environment to his advantage.

Al Mansi was a quick learner, and he soon became adept at disappearing into the background, his presence barely noticeable to those around him.

As their training progressed, Sarab began to see something special in Al Mansi. He had a natural talent for this type of work, and he had the dedication and discipline to master it completely.

In time, Sarab knew that Al Mansi would become one of the best assassins the League had ever seen. And with each passing day, Al Mansi's skills continued to grow stronger, his resolve to succeed unbreakable.

Together, they continued their training, honing their skills and preparing for whatever challenges lay ahead. And as they worked, Al Mansi knew that he was one step closer to achieving his ultimate goal – to become a true master of the art of killing.
During his time with Sarab, Al Mansi truly learn the ways of Ninjutsu. Deception, silent walking, subterfuge, theatrality. They becames his weapons as much as the Kris Broadsword he so often favored.

Under Sarab's guidance, Al Mansi had truly come to master the ways of Ninjutsu. He had learned the art of deception, of silent walking, of subterfuge and theatrality. These skills had become just as important as the kris broadsword he had always favored, and he knew that they would be his key to success in many of the missions that lay ahead.

Al Mansi spent countless hours honing his skills, perfecting his ability to blend into the shadows and become one with his surroundings. He learned how to use makeup to change his appearance, how to scale walls and move effortlessly through tight spaces, and how to set traps that would leave his enemies baffled and disoriented.

With each new skill he learned, Al Mansi felt his confidence growing. He knew that he was now a true master of the art of killing, ready to face any challenge that came his way.

And as he worked and trained alongside Sarab, he felt a deep sense of gratitude and respect for his mentor. Sarab had given him the tools he needed to succeed, and he knew that without him he would never have come so far.

As they continued their training, Al Mansi began to see the world in a whole new way. He noticed the tiniest details, heard the faintest sounds, and could anticipate the movements of his opponents with uncanny accuracy.

It was a thrilling and exciting time for Al Mansi, and he knew that he was now well on his way to becoming a legendary assassin – a master of the art of death, feared and respected by all who knew him.
Month passed and Ta-er al-Sahfer, Nyssa and Al Mansi became a team.

After months of training and preparation, Ta-er al-Sahfer, Nyssa, and Al Mansi were now a team, ready to take on any mission that came their way. They had each proven themselves as skilled warriors and assassins, and they knew that together they would be virtually unstoppable.

Over the next several weeks, the team embarked on a series of missions, each one more challenging than the last. They traveled to remote locations around the world, infiltrating top-secret facilities and taking out high-profile targets with ease.

And as they worked together, they developed a deep camaraderie and respect for one another. Nyssa was impressed by Al Mansi's immense dedication and skill, and Ta-er al-Sahfer saw in him a fierce determination and a will to succeed.

For his part, Al Mansi felt a deep sense of gratitude towards his teammates. They had welcomed him into their fold and helped him to become the best assassin he could possibly be. He had never felt more alive than he did when working alongside them, reveling in the thrill of the chase and the satisfaction of a mission accomplished.

Together, they were a force to be reckoned with – a team of highly skilled assassins who worked together in perfect harmony, taking out their targets with ruthless efficiency. And as they traveled the world, leaving a trail of destruction in their wake, they knew that they were making a difference – that they were fighting the good fight, and doing so with honor and dignity.

As time passes, their team became the stuff of legends even by league standards. The name given to their team was demonstrating this. They were The Three Shadows. Each with a unique style who could offer support to his two teammates.

As time passed, The Three Shadows became the stuff of legends even by League of Assassins' standards. Their skill and efficiency in completing missions were unparalleled, and their reputation spread throughout the organization. They became known as the ultimate team for any mission, and they accepted only the most difficult and dangerous tasks.

Their name was given to them by those who had heard of their exploits, a testament to their skills and the way they moved through the world undetected. Each member had a unique style of their own, but when they worked together, they moved as one, anticipating each other's movements and offering support where it was needed.

Ta-er al-Sahfer was known for her fierce hand-to-hand combat skills, and she was a master of using the environment to her advantage. Nyssa, on the other hand, was an expert in long-range weapons like the bow and arrow, and she could take out targets from a distance with remarkable accuracy. And then there was Al Mansi, who had perfected the art of deception and subterfuge, making him the perfect assassin for stealth operations.

Together, they were an unstoppable force, feared by their enemies and revered by their allies. Whether they were taking out high-profile targets or retrieving valuable artifacts, they completed their missions with ruthless efficiency, leaving behind no trace of their involvement.

And as they continued to work together, building on their successes and growing even more skilled in their craft, they knew that they were part of something special – a team that was more than the sum of its parts, a force that demanded respect and commanded attention wherever they went. They were The Three Shadows, and they knew that nothing could stand in their way.

They gain the notice of Ras AlGhul who, with his more than keen eyes, realized that the three were dancing around each other, or more precisely, Nyssa and her partner the former Sara Lance, were now dancing around Al Mansi as much as Al Mansi was dancing around them.

As The Three Shadows continued to work together, their legend grew, eventually catching the attention of Ras Al Ghul himself. The leader of the League of Assassins was known for his keen insight and analytical mind, and it wasn't long before he began to notice something unusual about the team.

Watching from afar, Ras Al Ghul saw that Nyssa and Ta-er al-Sahfer were dancing around Al Mansi just as much as he was dancing around them. They had developed a unique bond, a connection that went beyond the simple dynamics of a mission team.

Ras Al Ghul realized that he was witnessing something truly special – a team that had become more than just a group of assassins working together. They had become a family, each member supporting and complementing the others in a way that was unparalleled.

Ras Al Ghul knew that this was something that could not be ignored. He recognized that The Three Shadows had achieved something extraordinary, and he saw in them the potential to take the League of Assassins to new heights.

And so, he summoned the team to his presence, eager to see them for himself. When they arrived, he wasted no time in expressing his admiration, congratulating them on their many successes and praising their unique approach to their work.

But Ras Al Ghul was not one to rest on his laurels. He had big plans for The Three Shadows, plans that would take their skills to the next level and make them an even more formidable force within the League.

With his guidance and support, The Three Shadows would become an even greater force to be reckoned with, feared and respected by all who stood in their way. They had already achieved so much, but there was still so much more that they could do – together, as a team.

The Three Shadows as a rundown with the Longbow Hunter, a fierce team of assassins that the league tries to avoid. Their fight end in a draw of sort and the League of Assassin’s team can’t help notice their targets escape. They return to Nanda Parbat with shame in their eyes and the resolve to become even better fighters than before.

The Three Shadows were sent on a mission to take down the Longbow Hunter, a team of assassins who were feared throughout the League. The Hunter was known for their fearlessness and their success rate in completing their missions, and The Three Shadows knew that they would have their work cut out for them.

The two teams clashed in a fierce battle that lasted for hours. The fighting was intense, with both sides refusing to back down. Blow for blow they fought, trading blows with skill beyond anything they had ever faced before.

In the end, the fight ended in a draw of sorts, with both teams left battered and exhausted. However, the Longbow Hunter was able to escape, leaving The Three Shadows with a bitter sense of defeat.

As they returned to Nanda Parbat, the team was filled with shame and disappointment. They had failed to complete their mission, and they knew that they had let down their teammates and their leaders.

But even in the midst of their shame, they knew that they had to keep pushing forward. They had to become even better fighters than before, stronger and more skilled than ever. The team began to train even harder, pushing themselves to their limits and beyond.

And the results soon began to show. The Three Shadows became even more skilled in their craft, honing their abilities in hand-to-hand combat, long-range weaponry, and the art of concealment. They knew that the path ahead of them would be long and difficult, but they were determined to succeed, to become the greatest team of assassins that the League had ever seen.

And with each passing day, they grew stronger, more skilled, more determined. They were The Three Shadows, and they would stop at nothing to achieve greatness.

Set juste after the end of Season 1. As years passed, Al Mansi presence helped Sara not lost herself in the killing. And when news of the undertakings, of the Glades of Starling City reach them, he offered support. « Maybe I could go there in your place. Make sure your loved one there are self and learn how it was possible that someone such as Al Saher could betray us without anyone noticing. It could be useful in the futur too. Nysa what do you think your father will say about it ? ».

As the years passed, Al Mansi and Sara grew closer with each passing day. Al Mansi had become more than just a partner to her. He had become a friend, a confidant, and someone she trusted more than anyone else in the world.

As news of the devastation in the Glades of Starling City reached them, Sara was filled with a sense of sorrow and helplessness. She longed to go there herself, to see the damage firsthand and lend her skills to the rescue efforts. But she knew that her responsibilities to the League came first.

That's when Al Mansi stepped in.

"Maybe I could go there in your place," he offered. "Make sure your loved ones there are safe and learn how it was possible that someone such as Al Saher could betray us without anyone noticing. It could be useful in the future too. Nysa, what do you think your father will say about it?"

Nyssa considered the question for
